teachingPoint: Meta-arbitration must register the meta-rule on the same axis it arbitrates. A meta-rule that 'judges judgment' is itself judged by structural conformance to the panel's roles.
positiveExample: FLOW-35 META-CF-01: meta-arbiter scoped to scope_isolation panel role, with explicit input contract = (verdicts: PanelVerdict[]) and output contract = MetaVerdict.
negativeExample: Meta-rule that fires across all panel roles with no role-axis declaration: every arbiter triggers it, infinite regress at runtime.
discriminatingConstraint: Meta-rules name the role axis they govern. There is no global meta-arbiter; each lives on a single role.
keywords: meta arbiter role axis panel verdict scope isolation

teachingPoint: OVERRIDE/DEFER decisions from a meta-arbiter must be paired with a written reason captured in the audit trail. Without a reason, the decision cannot be rolled back deterministically nor used as DPO triple input.
positiveExample: MetaVerdict { decision: 'OVERRIDE', reason: 'panel role 8 (goal_delivery) raised non-negotiable goal-completeness violation', overriddenRoles: ['business_logic'] } stored to xiigen-decision-graph with full context.
negativeExample: Returning { decision: 'OVERRIDE' } with no reason: arbitration loop succeeds, but downstream rerun cannot reproduce the override and the DPO triple is uselessly ambiguous.
discriminatingConstraint: Every override carries a structural reason field; missing reason is a CF-violation, not a warning.
keywords: override defer audit reason rollback DPO

teachingPoint: Meta-arbiters run AFTER role panels return their verdicts, never AS a panel role. Mixing tiers makes confidence calibration meaningless because the meta-judgment becomes one vote among peers.
positiveExample: Pipeline ordering: AF-9 produces verdicts → MetaArbiter consumes verdicts → arbitration loop proceeds. MetaArbiter never participates in the panel itself.
negativeExample: Treating meta-arbiter as a Role 11 alongside Role 1-10: panel weight per role is implicitly equal, so meta-vote ties with goal_delivery.
discriminatingConstraint: Meta-arbitration tier is post-panel, never in-panel. Pipeline position is the structural marker.
keywords: tier post-panel meta arbitration pipeline AF-9 verdict
